title: No inhibition rules — database outage triggers 30 symptomatic alerts
code_snippet:
# Alertmanager config
route:
  receiver: pagerduty-oncall
  group_by: ['alertname', 'severity']
  group_wait: 30s
  group_interval: 5m
  routes:
    - match:
        severity: critical
      receiver: pagerduty-oncall
    - match:
        severity: warning
      receiver: team-slack

# No inhibit_rules section at all

# When PostgreSQL goes down:
# - DatabaseDown fires (1 alert) → pages on-call
# - HighErrorRate fires from 6 dependent services → 6 more pages
# - SLOBurnRateHigh fires for all 6 services → 6 more pages
# - CacheMissRateHigh fires (Redis fallback to DB fails) → 1 more page
# Total: 14 pages for 1 root cause, on-call overwhelmed
expected_feedback: Standard: Alertmanager config has proper routing and grouping but no inhibit_rules. When PostgreSQL goes down, DatabaseDown fires correctly, but all dependent services also fire HighErrorRate and SLOBurnRateHigh — generating 14 separate pages for one root cause. On-call wastes time triaging redundant alerts instead of fixing the database. Fix: add inhibit_rules — DatabaseDown should suppress HighErrorRate and SLOBurnRateHigh on dependent services, scoped by equal: ['cluster', 'namespace']. This reduces 14 pages to 1 actionable page for the root cause.
